modules.comments:
Construction, arithmetic, and inspection of `Duration` values.

A `Duration` represents a length of time as a combination of date and time
fields (years through nanoseconds). Key things to know:

- **Same-sign rule**: all components must be zero or share the same sign.
  Constructing `{ years: 1, months: -1 }` will fail.
- **Unbalanced by default**: `{ hours: 36 }` stays as-is and does not
  auto-convert to `{ days: 1, hours: 12 }`. Use `round` to rebalance.
- **Eq is structural**: `hours 1.0 /= minutes 60.0` because `Eq` compares
  component-by-component, not total elapsed time.
- **Calendar operations need context**: `round`, `total`, and `add`/`subtract`
  may return `Nothing` when calendar units (years, months, weeks) are involved,
  because their real-world length depends on a reference date.

declarations.comments:
All fields of a `Duration`. Every field defaults to `0.0` — set only the
fields you need.

All non-zero fields must share the same sign, or construction will fail.
declarations.title: DurationFields

declarations.comments:
All fields set to `0.0`. Use record update syntax:

```purescript
duration (defaultDurationFields { hours = 2.0, minutes = 30.0 })
```
declarations.title: defaultDurationFields

declarations.comments:
Construct a `Duration` from explicit fields. Returns `Nothing` if the
fields have mixed signs (e.g. positive years with negative months).
declarations.title: duration

declarations.comments:
Parse an ISO 8601 duration string (e.g. `"PT1H30M"`, `"P1Y2M3D"`).
Returns `Nothing` for invalid strings.

Note: subsecond components are collapsed during serialization, so
`fromString (toString d)` may produce a structurally different (but equal)
duration. For example, `{ milliseconds: 1000 }` serializes to `"PT1S"` and
parses back as `{ seconds: 1 }`.
declarations.title: fromString

declarations.comments:
Create a duration of the given number of years. Always succeeds for
finite values.
declarations.title: years

declarations.comments: Create a duration of the given number of months.
declarations.title: months

declarations.comments: Create a duration of the given number of weeks.
declarations.title: weeks

declarations.comments: Create a duration of the given number of days.
declarations.title: days

declarations.comments: Create a duration of the given number of hours.
declarations.title: hours

declarations.comments: Create a duration of the given number of minutes.
declarations.title: minutes

declarations.comments: Create a duration of the given number of seconds.
declarations.title: seconds

declarations.comments: Create a duration of the given number of milliseconds.
declarations.title: milliseconds

declarations.comments: Create a duration of the given number of microseconds.
declarations.title: microseconds

declarations.comments: Create a duration of the given number of nanoseconds.
declarations.title: nanoseconds

declarations.comments: The sign of the duration: `1` for positive, `-1` for negative, `0` for blank.
declarations.title: sign

declarations.comments: Whether all fields are zero.
declarations.title: blank

declarations.comments:
Add two durations. Returns `Nothing` if the durations involve calendar
units (years, months, weeks) — use date arithmetic with a reference date
instead: `startDate # add dur1 >>= add dur2 >>= \d -> since' d startDate`.
declarations.title: add

declarations.comments: Subtract one duration from another. Same calendar-unit caveats as `add`.
declarations.title: subtract

declarations.comments:
Return the duration with all component signs flipped.
`negated (negated d) == d` for all durations.
declarations.title: negated

declarations.comments:
Return the duration with all components made non-negative.
`abs d == abs (negated d)` for all durations.
declarations.title: abs

declarations.comments:
Options for `round`. The `roundingIncrement` must evenly divide the
next-larger unit's maximum (e.g. for minutes: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 10,
12, 15, 20, 30).
declarations.title: DurationRoundOptions

declarations.comments:
Round and/or rebalance a duration. Returns `Nothing` if calendar units
are involved (this binding does not support the `relativeTo` option).

To rebalance without rounding, set `largestUnit` to the desired largest
unit:

```purescript
round (defaultDurationRoundOptions { largestUnit = TimeU Hours }) (minutes 90.0)
-- Just (Duration PT2H) — rebalanced from 90 minutes to 1h30m, then rounded to hours
```
declarations.title: round

declarations.comments:
Compute the total duration in the given unit as a fractional number.
Returns `Nothing` if calendar units are involved (this binding does not
support the `relativeTo` option).

```purescript
total (TimeU Minutes) (hours 1.0)  -- Just 60.0
```
declarations.title: total

declarations.comments: Serialize to an ISO 8601 duration string (e.g. `"PT1H30M"`).
declarations.title: toString

modules.name: Temporal.Duration

declarations.comments:
A length of time expressed as a combination of date and time components
(years, months, weeks, days, hours, minutes, seconds, milliseconds,
microseconds, nanoseconds). All components must share the same sign.

Durations are **unbalanced** — `{ hours: 36 }` is not automatically
converted to `{ days: 1, hours: 12 }`. Use `round` to balance.

`Eq` compares component-by-component (not total elapsed time), so
`{ hours: 1 }` is not equal to `{ minutes: 60 }`.
declarations.title: Duration

modules.comments:
Construction, arithmetic, and inspection of `Instant` values.

An `Instant` represents an exact moment on the UTC timeline with nanosecond
precision, independent of any time zone or calendar.

Key things to know:

- **Time-only arithmetic**: `add` and `subtract` only accept durations with
  time components (hours and below). Passing years, months, weeks, or days
  returns `Nothing`, because their real-world length varies with time zone
  and calendar. Convert to `ZonedDateTime` first for calendar arithmetic.
- **Parsing requires UTC offset**: `fromString "2024-03-15T10:30:00"`
  returns `Nothing` — you must include `Z` or an offset like `+00:00`.
- **Diff returns time-only**: `since`/`until` return durations with `Hours`
  as the default largest unit. The result may have hours exceeding 24.

declarations.comments:
Construct an `Instant` from nanoseconds since the Unix epoch.
Returns `Nothing` if the value is outside the representable range
(approximately ±10^8 days from epoch).
declarations.title: fromEpochNanoseconds

declarations.comments:
Construct an `Instant` from milliseconds since the Unix epoch.
Returns `Nothing` if the value is outside the representable range.

The fractional part is truncated (not rounded).
declarations.title: fromEpochMilliseconds

declarations.comments:
Parse an ISO 8601 string with a UTC offset (e.g. `"2024-03-15T10:30:00Z"`,
`"2024-03-15T10:30:00+05:30"`). Returns `Nothing` for invalid strings or
strings without a UTC offset.
declarations.title: fromString

declarations.comments: Milliseconds since the Unix epoch. This may lose sub-millisecond precision.
declarations.title: getEpochMilliseconds

declarations.comments: Nanoseconds since the Unix epoch (full precision).
declarations.title: getEpochNanoseconds

declarations.comments:
Add a time-only duration. Returns `Nothing` if the duration contains
date components (years, months, weeks, or days) or if the result would
be outside the representable range.
declarations.title: add

declarations.comments: Subtract a time-only duration. Same restrictions as `add`.
declarations.title: subtract

declarations.comments:
Options for `since` and `until`. Only time units (hours and below) are
allowed. Default: largest unit is `Hours`, smallest unit is `Nanoseconds`.
declarations.title: InstantDiffOptions

declarations.comments:
`since opts a b` returns the duration from `b` to `a`
(i.e., `a.since(b)` in JS). The result is positive when `a` is after `b`.
declarations.title: since

declarations.comments:
`until opts a b` returns the duration from `a` to `b`
(i.e., `a.until(b)` in JS). The result is positive when `b` is after `a`.
declarations.title: until

declarations.comments: `since' a b` — duration from `b` to `a` with default options.
declarations.title: since'

declarations.comments: `until' a b` — duration from `a` to `b` with default options.
declarations.title: until'

declarations.comments: Options for `round`. Only time units are allowed.
declarations.title: InstantRoundOptions

declarations.comments: Round the instant to the given precision.
declarations.title: round

declarations.comments:
Convert to a `ZonedDateTime` in the given IANA time zone using the
ISO 8601 calendar.

```purescript
toZonedDateTimeISO "America/New_York" instant
```
declarations.title: toZonedDateTimeISO

declarations.comments: Serialize to an ISO 8601 string with `Z` suffix (e.g. `"2024-03-15T10:30:00Z"`).
declarations.title: toString

modules.name: Temporal.Instant

declarations.comments:
A date and time in a specific time zone, with full awareness of UTC offset
and DST transitions (e.g. 2024-03-15T10:30:00-04:00[America/New_York]).

This is the richest Temporal type. Use it for scheduling future events
where wall-clock time should be preserved even if DST rules change.
For past events or timestamps, `Instant` is usually sufficient.
declarations.title: ZonedDateTime

declarations.comments:
An exact moment on the UTC timeline with nanosecond precision,
independent of any time zone or calendar.

Use `Instant` for timestamps, event logs, and any case where you need
an unambiguous point in time. Convert to `ZonedDateTime` when you need
wall-clock representation in a specific time zone.
declarations.title: Instant

declarations.comments:
A length of time expressed as a combination of date and time components
(years, months, weeks, days, hours, minutes, seconds, milliseconds,
microseconds, nanoseconds). All components must share the same sign.

Durations are **unbalanced** — `{ hours: 36 }` is not automatically
converted to `{ days: 1, hours: 12 }`. Use `round` to balance.

`Eq` compares component-by-component (not total elapsed time), so
`{ hours: 1 }` is not equal to `{ minutes: 60 }`.
declarations.title: Duration

modules.comments:
Option types used across Temporal operations.

These ADTs represent the various option enums from the TC39 Temporal
specification. They are converted to JavaScript strings before being passed
to the FFI.

declarations.comments:
Rounding modes matching TC39 Temporal specification.

- `HalfExpand` (default) — round half toward positive infinity ("normal" rounding)
- `Ceil` — round toward positive infinity
- `Floor` — round toward negative infinity
- `Trunc` — round toward zero
- `Expand` — round away from zero
- `HalfCeil` — round half toward positive infinity
- `HalfFloor` — round half toward negative infinity
- `HalfTrunc` — round half toward zero
- `HalfEven` — round half to even (banker's rounding)
declarations.title: RoundingMode

declarations.comments:
How to handle out-of-range field values (e.g. day 31 in a 30-day month).

- `Constrain` — clamp to the nearest valid value (e.g. day 31 → day 30)
- `Reject` — fail (return `Nothing`)

Most Temporal constructors default to `Constrain`.
declarations.title: Overflow

declarations.comments:
How to resolve wall-clock times that are ambiguous or non-existent due to
DST transitions.

- `Compatible` (default) — acts like `Later` for gaps (spring forward) and
  `Earlier` for ambiguities (fall back). This matches the behavior of
  legacy `Date`.
- `Earlier` — pick the earlier of two possible instants
- `Later` — pick the later of two possible instants
- `RejectDisambiguation` — fail for ambiguous or non-existent times
declarations.title: Disambiguation

declarations.comments:
How to resolve conflicts between an explicit UTC offset in a string and
the IANA timezone's rules for that instant.

- `Use` — trust the offset, ignore timezone rules
- `Ignore` — trust the timezone, recompute the offset
- `Prefer` — use the offset if it's valid for the timezone, otherwise
  recompute (good for `with` operations)
- `RejectOffset` — fail if offset and timezone disagree (good for parsing
  untrusted input)
declarations.title: OffsetDisambiguation

declarations.comments: Direction for `getTimeZoneTransition` queries.
declarations.title: TransitionDirection

declarations.comments: Units that apply only to date components.
declarations.title: DateUnit

declarations.comments: Units that apply only to time components.
declarations.title: TimeUnit

declarations.comments: Combined date+time units for operations that accept either.
declarations.title: DateTimeUnit

modules.name: Temporal.Internal.Options

modules.comments:
Internal module defining foreign Temporal types with their core typeclass
instances. These types are re-exported from their respective modules
(e.g. `Temporal.Duration`, `Temporal.Instant`, etc.).

declarations.comments:
A length of time expressed as a combination of date and time components
(years, months, weeks, days, hours, minutes, seconds, milliseconds,
microseconds, nanoseconds). All components must share the same sign.

Durations are **unbalanced** — `{ hours: 36 }` is not automatically
converted to `{ days: 1, hours: 12 }`. Use `round` to balance.

`Eq` compares component-by-component (not total elapsed time), so
`{ hours: 1 }` is not equal to `{ minutes: 60 }`.
declarations.title: Duration

declarations.comments:
An exact moment on the UTC timeline with nanosecond precision,
independent of any time zone or calendar.

Use `Instant` for timestamps, event logs, and any case where you need
an unambiguous point in time. Convert to `ZonedDateTime` when you need
wall-clock representation in a specific time zone.
declarations.title: Instant

declarations.comments:
A calendar date without a time component or time zone (e.g. 2024-03-15).

Use `PlainDate` for birthdays, holidays, and other dates where time of day
is irrelevant. Do not use for scheduling events — use `ZonedDateTime` instead.
declarations.title: PlainDate

declarations.comments:
A wall-clock time without a date or time zone (e.g. 10:30:00).
Range: 00:00:00.000000000 to 23:59:59.999999999.

Arithmetic wraps at midnight with no overflow indication.
declarations.title: PlainTime

declarations.comments:
A calendar date and wall-clock time without a time zone
(e.g. 2024-03-15T10:30:00).

Use `PlainDateTime` for events whose time zone is tracked separately,
or for wall-clock displays. For unambiguous moments in time, use `Instant`
or `ZonedDateTime` instead.
declarations.title: PlainDateTime

declarations.comments:
A year and month without a day component (e.g. 2024-03).

Useful for representing months in a calendar UI or credit card expiry dates.
declarations.title: PlainYearMonth

declarations.comments:
A month and day without a year component (e.g. 12-25).

Useful for recurring annual dates like birthdays or holidays.
declarations.title: PlainMonthDay

declarations.comments:
A date and time in a specific time zone, with full awareness of UTC offset
and DST transitions (e.g. 2024-03-15T10:30:00-04:00[America/New_York]).

This is the richest Temporal type. Use it for scheduling future events
where wall-clock time should be preserved even if DST rules change.
For past events or timestamps, `Instant` is usually sufficient.
declarations.title: ZonedDateTime

modules.name: Temporal.Internal.Types

modules.comments:
Access the current date, time, and time zone from the system clock.

All functions in this module perform effects (reading the system clock).
Functions without an `In` suffix use the system's default time zone.
Functions with an `In` suffix take an IANA time zone name.

declarations.comments: The current instant (exact moment on the UTC timeline).
declarations.title: instant

declarations.comments: The current date and time in the system's default time zone.
declarations.title: zonedDateTimeISO

declarations.comments: The current date and time in the given IANA time zone.
declarations.title: zonedDateTimeISOIn

declarations.comments: The current date and time (no timezone) in the system's default time zone.
declarations.title: plainDateTimeISO

declarations.comments: The current date and time (no timezone) in the given IANA time zone.
declarations.title: plainDateTimeISOIn

declarations.comments: The current date in the system's default time zone.
declarations.title: plainDateISO

declarations.comments: The current date in the given IANA time zone.
declarations.title: plainDateISOIn

declarations.comments: The current time in the system's default time zone.
declarations.title: plainTimeISO

declarations.comments: The current time in the given IANA time zone.
declarations.title: plainTimeISOIn

declarations.comments: The system's default IANA time zone identifier (e.g. `"America/New_York"`).
declarations.title: timeZoneId

modules.name: Temporal.Now

modules.comments:
Construction, arithmetic, and inspection of `PlainDate` values.

A `PlainDate` represents a calendar date without a time component or time
zone (e.g. 2024-03-15).

Key things to know:

- **Default overflow is constrain**: `plainDate 2024 2 31` silently
  produces February 29 (clamped), not `Nothing`. Use `plainDateWith Reject`
  for strict validation.
- **Arithmetic order matters**: when adding a multi-component duration,
  fields are applied in order (years, then months, then weeks+days) with
  clamping after each step. `add { months: 1, days: 31 }` on Jan 31
  gives Mar 31 (Jan 31 + 1 month = Feb 28, + 31 days = Mar 31), which
  differs from adding months and days in separate steps.

declarations.comments:
Construct a `PlainDate` from year, month, and day. Out-of-range days are
**constrained** (clamped) to the nearest valid value. For example,
`plainDate 2024 2 31` yields February 29 (2024 is a leap year).

Returns `Nothing` only for truly invalid values (e.g. month 13).
Use `plainDateWith Reject` if you want strict validation.
declarations.title: plainDate

declarations.comments:
Construct a `PlainDate` with explicit overflow handling.

- `Constrain`: clamp out-of-range values (default Temporal behavior)
- `Reject`: return `Nothing` for any out-of-range value
declarations.title: plainDateWith

declarations.comments:
Parse an ISO 8601 date string (e.g. `"2024-03-15"`).
Returns `Nothing` for invalid strings.
declarations.title: fromString

declarations.comments: Calendar year (e.g. 2024). Can be negative for BCE dates.
declarations.title: getYear

declarations.comments: Month of the year (1–12).
declarations.title: getMonth

declarations.comments: Day of the month (1–31).
declarations.title: getDay

declarations.comments: Day of the week (1 = Monday, 7 = Sunday), per ISO 8601.
declarations.title: getDayOfWeek

declarations.comments: Day of the year (1–366).
declarations.title: getDayOfYear

declarations.comments: ISO 8601 week number (1–53).
declarations.title: getWeekOfYear

declarations.comments:
The year that corresponds to `getWeekOfYear`. May differ from `getYear`
at year boundaries (e.g. Dec 31 may be in week 1 of the next year).
declarations.title: getYearOfWeek

declarations.comments: Number of days in this date's month (28–31).
declarations.title: getDaysInMonth

declarations.comments: Always 7.
declarations.title: getDaysInWeek

declarations.comments: Number of days in this date's year (365 or 366).
declarations.title: getDaysInYear

declarations.comments: Always 12 for the ISO calendar.
declarations.title: getMonthsInYear

declarations.comments: Whether this date's year is a leap year.
declarations.title: getInLeapYear

declarations.comments:
Create a modified copy with the given fields. The `Overflow` option
controls how out-of-range values are handled.
declarations.title: with

declarations.comments:
Add a duration to this date. Returns `Nothing` on overflow.
Time components of the duration are ignored.
declarations.title: add

declarations.comments:
Subtract a duration from this date. Returns `Nothing` on overflow.
Time components of the duration are ignored.
declarations.title: subtract

declarations.comments:
Options for `since` and `until`. Default: largest unit is `Years`,
smallest unit is `Days`.
declarations.title: PlainDateDiffOptions

declarations.comments:
`since opts a b` returns the duration from `b` to `a`
(i.e., `a.since(b)` in JS). The result is positive when `a` is after `b`.
declarations.title: since

declarations.comments:
`until opts a b` returns the duration from `a` to `b`
(i.e., `a.until(b)` in JS). The result is positive when `b` is after `a`.
declarations.title: until

declarations.comments: `since' a b` — duration from `b` to `a` with default options.
declarations.title: since'

declarations.comments: `until' a b` — duration from `a` to `b` with default options.
declarations.title: until'

declarations.comments: Combine this date with a time to produce a `PlainDateTime`.
declarations.title: toPlainDateTime

declarations.comments: Drop the day component, keeping year and month.
declarations.title: toPlainYearMonth

declarations.comments: Drop the year component, keeping month and day.
declarations.title: toPlainMonthDay

declarations.comments:
Combine this date with a time and IANA time zone to produce a
`ZonedDateTime`. DST disambiguation uses the default `"compatible"` mode.
declarations.title: toZonedDateTime

declarations.comments: Serialize to an ISO 8601 date string (e.g. `"2024-03-15"`).
declarations.title: toString

modules.name: Temporal.PlainDate

declarations.comments:
A date and time in a specific time zone, with full awareness of UTC offset
and DST transitions (e.g. 2024-03-15T10:30:00-04:00[America/New_York]).

This is the richest Temporal type. Use it for scheduling future events
where wall-clock time should be preserved even if DST rules change.
For past events or timestamps, `Instant` is usually sufficient.
declarations.title: ZonedDateTime

declarations.comments:
A year and month without a day component (e.g. 2024-03).

Useful for representing months in a calendar UI or credit card expiry dates.
declarations.title: PlainYearMonth

declarations.comments:
A wall-clock time without a date or time zone (e.g. 10:30:00).
Range: 00:00:00.000000000 to 23:59:59.999999999.

Arithmetic wraps at midnight with no overflow indication.
declarations.title: PlainTime

declarations.comments:
A month and day without a year component (e.g. 12-25).

Useful for recurring annual dates like birthdays or holidays.
declarations.title: PlainMonthDay

declarations.comments:
A calendar date and wall-clock time without a time zone
(e.g. 2024-03-15T10:30:00).

Use `PlainDateTime` for events whose time zone is tracked separately,
or for wall-clock displays. For unambiguous moments in time, use `Instant`
or `ZonedDateTime` instead.
declarations.title: PlainDateTime

declarations.comments:
A calendar date without a time component or time zone (e.g. 2024-03-15).

Use `PlainDate` for birthdays, holidays, and other dates where time of day
is irrelevant. Do not use for scheduling events — use `ZonedDateTime` instead.
declarations.title: PlainDate

declarations.comments:
A length of time expressed as a combination of date and time components
(years, months, weeks, days, hours, minutes, seconds, milliseconds,
microseconds, nanoseconds). All components must share the same sign.

Durations are **unbalanced** — `{ hours: 36 }` is not automatically
converted to `{ days: 1, hours: 12 }`. Use `round` to balance.

`Eq` compares component-by-component (not total elapsed time), so
`{ hours: 1 }` is not equal to `{ minutes: 60 }`.
declarations.title: Duration

modules.comments:
Construction, arithmetic, and inspection of `PlainDateTime` values.

A `PlainDateTime` represents a calendar date and wall-clock time without a
time zone (e.g. 2024-03-15T10:30:00). It combines the semantics of
`PlainDate` and `PlainTime`.

Use `PlainDateTime` when you need date+time but the time zone is tracked
separately or is irrelevant. For unambiguous moments in time, use `Instant`
or `ZonedDateTime`.

declarations.comments:
All fields of a `PlainDateTime`. Date fields default to year 0, month 1,
day 1. Time fields default to 0.
declarations.title: PlainDateTimeFields

declarations.comments:
Default fields: 0000-01-01T00:00:00.000000000. Use record update syntax:

```purescript
plainDateTime (defaultPlainDateTimeFields { year = 2024, month = 3, day = 15, hour = 10 })
```
declarations.title: defaultPlainDateTimeFields

declarations.comments: Construct a `PlainDateTime` from fields. Returns `Nothing` for invalid values.
declarations.title: plainDateTime

declarations.comments:
Parse an ISO 8601 date-time string (e.g. `"2024-03-15T10:30:00"`).
Returns `Nothing` for invalid strings. Time zone information, if present,
is ignored.
declarations.title: fromString

declarations.comments: Calendar year.
declarations.title: getYear

declarations.comments: Month of the year (1–12).
declarations.title: getMonth

declarations.comments: Day of the month (1–31).
declarations.title: getDay

declarations.comments: Hour of the day (0–23).
declarations.title: getHour

declarations.comments: Minute of the hour (0–59).
declarations.title: getMinute

declarations.comments: Second of the minute (0–59).
declarations.title: getSecond

declarations.comments: Millisecond (0–999).
declarations.title: getMillisecond

declarations.comments: Microsecond (0–999).
declarations.title: getMicrosecond

declarations.comments: Nanosecond (0–999).
declarations.title: getNanosecond

declarations.comments: Day of the week (1 = Monday, 7 = Sunday), per ISO 8601.
declarations.title: getDayOfWeek

declarations.comments: Day of the year (1–366).
declarations.title: getDayOfYear

declarations.comments: ISO 8601 week number (1–53).
declarations.title: getWeekOfYear

declarations.comments: The year that corresponds to `getWeekOfYear`.
declarations.title: getYearOfWeek

declarations.comments: Number of days in this date-time's month (28–31).
declarations.title: getDaysInMonth

declarations.comments: Always 7.
declarations.title: getDaysInWeek

declarations.comments: Number of days in this date-time's year (365 or 366).
declarations.title: getDaysInYear

declarations.comments: Always 12 for the ISO calendar.
declarations.title: getMonthsInYear

declarations.comments: Whether this date-time's year is a leap year.
declarations.title: getInLeapYear

declarations.comments:
Create a modified copy with the given fields. Returns `Nothing` if the
resulting date-time would be invalid.
declarations.title: with

declarations.comments: Replace the time component, keeping the date.
declarations.title: withPlainTime

declarations.comments: Add a duration. Returns `Nothing` on overflow.
declarations.title: add

declarations.comments: Subtract a duration. Returns `Nothing` on overflow.
declarations.title: subtract

declarations.comments:
Options for `since` and `until`. Default: largest unit is `Years`,
smallest unit is `Nanoseconds`.
declarations.title: PlainDateTimeDiffOptions

declarations.comments:
`since opts a b` returns the duration from `b` to `a`
(i.e., `a.since(b)` in JS). The result is positive when `a` is after `b`.
declarations.title: since

declarations.comments:
`until opts a b` returns the duration from `a` to `b`
(i.e., `a.until(b)` in JS). The result is positive when `b` is after `a`.
declarations.title: until

declarations.comments: `since' a b` — duration from `b` to `a` with default options.
declarations.title: since'

declarations.comments: `until' a b` — duration from `a` to `b` with default options.
declarations.title: until'

declarations.comments: Options for `round`.
declarations.title: PlainDateTimeRoundOptions

declarations.comments: Round the date-time to the given precision.
declarations.title: round

declarations.comments: Extract the date component.
declarations.title: toPlainDate

declarations.comments: Extract the time component.
declarations.title: toPlainTime

declarations.comments: Extract the year and month.
declarations.title: toPlainYearMonth

declarations.comments: Extract the month and day.
declarations.title: toPlainMonthDay

declarations.comments:
Convert to a `ZonedDateTime` in the given IANA time zone.
The `Disambiguation` option controls how ambiguous or non-existent
wall-clock times (during DST transitions) are resolved:

- `Compatible` (recommended default): acts like `Later` for gaps
  (spring forward) and `Earlier` for ambiguities (fall back)
- `Earlier`: pick the earlier of two possible instants
- `Later`: pick the later of two possible instants
- `RejectDisambiguation`: return the result but may be unpredictable
declarations.title: toZonedDateTime

declarations.comments: Serialize to an ISO 8601 string (e.g. `"2024-03-15T10:30:00"`).
declarations.title: toString

modules.name: Temporal.PlainDateTime

declarations.comments:
A date and time in a specific time zone, with full awareness of UTC offset
and DST transitions (e.g. 2024-03-15T10:30:00-04:00[America/New_York]).

This is the richest Temporal type. Use it for scheduling future events
where wall-clock time should be preserved even if DST rules change.
For past events or timestamps, `Instant` is usually sufficient.
declarations.title: ZonedDateTime

declarations.comments:
A year and month without a day component (e.g. 2024-03).

Useful for representing months in a calendar UI or credit card expiry dates.
declarations.title: PlainYearMonth

declarations.comments:
A wall-clock time without a date or time zone (e.g. 10:30:00).
Range: 00:00:00.000000000 to 23:59:59.999999999.

Arithmetic wraps at midnight with no overflow indication.
declarations.title: PlainTime

declarations.comments:
A month and day without a year component (e.g. 12-25).

Useful for recurring annual dates like birthdays or holidays.
declarations.title: PlainMonthDay

declarations.comments:
A calendar date and wall-clock time without a time zone
(e.g. 2024-03-15T10:30:00).

Use `PlainDateTime` for events whose time zone is tracked separately,
or for wall-clock displays. For unambiguous moments in time, use `Instant`
or `ZonedDateTime` instead.
declarations.title: PlainDateTime

declarations.comments:
A calendar date without a time component or time zone (e.g. 2024-03-15).

Use `PlainDate` for birthdays, holidays, and other dates where time of day
is irrelevant. Do not use for scheduling events — use `ZonedDateTime` instead.
declarations.title: PlainDate

declarations.comments:
A length of time expressed as a combination of date and time components
(years, months, weeks, days, hours, minutes, seconds, milliseconds,
microseconds, nanoseconds). All components must share the same sign.

Durations are **unbalanced** — `{ hours: 36 }` is not automatically
converted to `{ days: 1, hours: 12 }`. Use `round` to balance.

`Eq` compares component-by-component (not total elapsed time), so
`{ hours: 1 }` is not equal to `{ minutes: 60 }`.
declarations.title: Duration

modules.comments:
Construction and inspection of `PlainMonthDay` values.

A `PlainMonthDay` represents a month and day without a year component
(e.g. 12-25). Useful for recurring annual dates like birthdays or holidays.

Note: `PlainMonthDay` has `Eq` but not `Ord`, because ordering month-day
pairs without a year is ambiguous (leap day considerations).

declarations.comments:
Construct from month (1–12) and day (1–31). Returns `Nothing` for
invalid combinations (e.g. month 2, day 30).
declarations.title: plainMonthDay

declarations.comments:
Parse an ISO 8601 month-day string (e.g. `"--12-25"`).
Returns `Nothing` for invalid strings.
declarations.title: fromString

declarations.comments: Month code string (e.g. `"M12"` for December).
declarations.title: getMonthCode

declarations.comments: Day of the month (1–31).
declarations.title: getDay

declarations.comments:
Create a modified copy with the given month and day. Returns `Nothing`
if the result would be invalid.
declarations.title: with

declarations.comments:
Convert to a `PlainDate` by specifying a year. Out-of-range days are
**constrained** — e.g. Feb 29 in a non-leap year yields Feb 28.
Returns `Nothing` for truly invalid values.
declarations.title: toPlainDate

declarations.comments: Serialize to an ISO 8601 month-day string (e.g. `"--12-25"`).
declarations.title: toString

modules.name: Temporal.PlainMonthDay

declarations.comments:
A month and day without a year component (e.g. 12-25).

Useful for recurring annual dates like birthdays or holidays.
declarations.title: PlainMonthDay

declarations.comments:
A calendar date without a time component or time zone (e.g. 2024-03-15).

Use `PlainDate` for birthdays, holidays, and other dates where time of day
is irrelevant. Do not use for scheduling events — use `ZonedDateTime` instead.
declarations.title: PlainDate

modules.comments:
Construction, arithmetic, and inspection of `PlainTime` values.

A `PlainTime` represents a wall-clock time without a date or time zone
(e.g. 10:30:00). Range: 00:00:00.000000000 to 23:59:59.999999999.

Key things to know:

- **Wraps at midnight**: `add` and `subtract` silently wrap past midnight.
  Adding 2 hours to 23:30 gives 01:30 with no overflow indication.
- **Date units ignored**: if the duration passed to `add`/`subtract`
  contains years, months, weeks, or days, those fields are silently
  ignored — no error is raised.

declarations.comments: Fields for constructing a `PlainTime`. All fields default to `0`.
declarations.title: PlainTimeFields

declarations.comments:
All fields set to `0`. Use record update syntax:

```purescript
plainTime (defaultPlainTimeFields { hour = 14, minute = 30 })
```
declarations.title: defaultPlainTimeFields

declarations.comments:
Construct a `PlainTime` from fields. Returns `Nothing` if any field is
out of range (hour: 0-23, minute/second: 0-59, sub-second: 0-999).
declarations.title: plainTime

declarations.comments:
Parse an ISO 8601 time string (e.g. `"10:30:00"`, `"14:00"`).
Returns `Nothing` for invalid strings.
declarations.title: fromString

declarations.comments: Hour of the day (0–23).
declarations.title: getHour

declarations.comments: Minute of the hour (0–59).
declarations.title: getMinute

declarations.comments: Second of the minute (0–59).
declarations.title: getSecond

declarations.comments: Millisecond (0–999).
declarations.title: getMillisecond

declarations.comments: Microsecond (0–999).
declarations.title: getMicrosecond

declarations.comments: Nanosecond (0–999).
declarations.title: getNanosecond

declarations.comments:
Create a modified copy with the given fields. Returns `Nothing` if the
resulting time would be invalid.
declarations.title: with

declarations.comments:
Add a duration, wrapping at midnight. Date components (years, months,
weeks, days) in the duration are silently ignored.
declarations.title: add

declarations.comments:
Subtract a duration, wrapping at midnight. Date components (years, months,
weeks, days) in the duration are silently ignored.
declarations.title: subtract

declarations.comments:
Options for `since` and `until`. Default: largest unit is `Hours`,
smallest unit is `Nanoseconds`.
declarations.title: PlainTimeDiffOptions

declarations.comments:
`since opts a b` returns the duration from `b` to `a`
(i.e., `a.since(b)` in JS). The result is positive when `a` is after `b`.
declarations.title: since

declarations.comments:
`until opts a b` returns the duration from `a` to `b`
(i.e., `a.until(b)` in JS). The result is positive when `b` is after `a`.

`until opts a b == negated (since opts a b)` for all `a`, `b`.
declarations.title: until

declarations.comments: `since' a b` — duration from `b` to `a` with default options.
declarations.title: since'

declarations.comments: `until' a b` — duration from `a` to `b` with default options.
declarations.title: until'

declarations.comments: Options for `round`.
declarations.title: PlainTimeRoundOptions

declarations.comments: Round the time to the given precision.
declarations.title: round

declarations.comments: Combine this time with a date to produce a `PlainDateTime`.
declarations.title: toPlainDateTime

declarations.comments:
Serialize to an ISO 8601 time string (e.g. `"10:30:00"`).
Trailing zero components are omitted.
declarations.title: toString

modules.name: Temporal.PlainTime

declarations.comments:
A wall-clock time without a date or time zone (e.g. 10:30:00).
Range: 00:00:00.000000000 to 23:59:59.999999999.

Arithmetic wraps at midnight with no overflow indication.
declarations.title: PlainTime

declarations.comments:
A calendar date and wall-clock time without a time zone
(e.g. 2024-03-15T10:30:00).

Use `PlainDateTime` for events whose time zone is tracked separately,
or for wall-clock displays. For unambiguous moments in time, use `Instant`
or `ZonedDateTime` instead.
declarations.title: PlainDateTime

declarations.comments:
A calendar date without a time component or time zone (e.g. 2024-03-15).

Use `PlainDate` for birthdays, holidays, and other dates where time of day
is irrelevant. Do not use for scheduling events — use `ZonedDateTime` instead.
declarations.title: PlainDate

declarations.comments:
A length of time expressed as a combination of date and time components
(years, months, weeks, days, hours, minutes, seconds, milliseconds,
microseconds, nanoseconds). All components must share the same sign.

Durations are **unbalanced** — `{ hours: 36 }` is not automatically
converted to `{ days: 1, hours: 12 }`. Use `round` to balance.

`Eq` compares component-by-component (not total elapsed time), so
`{ hours: 1 }` is not equal to `{ minutes: 60 }`.
declarations.title: Duration

modules.comments:
Construction, arithmetic, and inspection of `PlainYearMonth` values.

A `PlainYearMonth` represents a year and month without a day component
(e.g. 2024-03). Useful for calendar month views, credit card expiry dates,
or any context where the specific day is irrelevant.

declarations.comments: Construct from year and month (1–12). Returns `Nothing` for invalid months.
declarations.title: plainYearMonth

declarations.comments:
Parse an ISO 8601 year-month string (e.g. `"2024-03"`).
Returns `Nothing` for invalid strings.

declarations.title: getYear

declarations.comments: Month of the year (1–12).
declarations.title: getMonth

declarations.comments: Month code string (e.g. `"M03"` for March). Useful for non-ISO calendars.
declarations.title: getMonthCode

declarations.comments: Number of days in this month (28–31).
declarations.title: getDaysInMonth

declarations.comments: Number of days in this year (365 or 366).
declarations.title: getDaysInYear

declarations.comments: Always 12 for the ISO calendar.
declarations.title: getMonthsInYear

declarations.comments: Whether this year is a leap year.
declarations.title: getInLeapYear

declarations.comments:
Create a modified copy with the given year and month. Returns `Nothing`
if the result would be invalid.

declarations.comments:
Add a duration (only year and month components are meaningful).
Returns `Nothing` on overflow.
declarations.title: add

declarations.comments: Subtract a duration. Returns `Nothing` on overflow.
declarations.title: subtract

declarations.comments:
Options for `since` and `until`. Default: largest unit is `Years`,
smallest unit is `Months`.
declarations.title: PlainYearMonthDiffOptions

declarations.comments: `since opts a b` returns the duration from `b` to `a`.
declarations.title: since

declarations.comments: `until opts a b` returns the duration from `a` to `b`.
declarations.title: until

declarations.comments: `since' a b` — duration from `b` to `a` with default options.
declarations.title: since'

declarations.comments: `until' a b` — duration from `a` to `b` with default options.
declarations.title: until'

declarations.comments:
Convert to a `PlainDate` by specifying a day. Out-of-range days are
**constrained** — e.g. `toPlainDate 31` on February yields Feb 28 or 29.
Returns `Nothing` for truly invalid values.
declarations.title: toPlainDate

declarations.comments: Serialize to an ISO 8601 year-month string (e.g. `"2024-03"`).
declarations.title: toString

modules.name: Temporal.PlainYearMonth

declarations.comments:
A year and month without a day component (e.g. 2024-03).

Useful for representing months in a calendar UI or credit card expiry dates.
declarations.title: PlainYearMonth

declarations.comments:
A calendar date without a time component or time zone (e.g. 2024-03-15).

Use `PlainDate` for birthdays, holidays, and other dates where time of day
is irrelevant. Do not use for scheduling events — use `ZonedDateTime` instead.
declarations.title: PlainDate

declarations.comments:
A length of time expressed as a combination of date and time components
(years, months, weeks, days, hours, minutes, seconds, milliseconds,
microseconds, nanoseconds). All components must share the same sign.

Durations are **unbalanced** — `{ hours: 36 }` is not automatically
converted to `{ days: 1, hours: 12 }`. Use `round` to balance.

`Eq` compares component-by-component (not total elapsed time), so
`{ hours: 1 }` is not equal to `{ minutes: 60 }`.
declarations.title: Duration

modules.comments:
Construction, arithmetic, and inspection of `ZonedDateTime` values.

A `ZonedDateTime` represents a date and time in a specific IANA time zone,
with full awareness of UTC offset and DST transitions. It is the richest
Temporal type.

Key things to know:

- **DST disambiguation**: when constructing from wall-clock time, the
  default `"compatible"` mode picks the later time for gaps (spring forward)
  and the earlier time for ambiguities (fall back).
- **Arithmetic splits date and time**: date components (years, months, days)
  are added as calendar arithmetic (wall-clock time preserved), while time
  components (hours, minutes, ...) are added as real elapsed time. Date
  components are processed first.
- **`startOfDay` may not be midnight**: in time zones where DST transitions
  occur at midnight (e.g. historically in Brazil), the start of day can be
  01:00 or another time.
- **Eq compares instant + timezone**: two `ZonedDateTime`s with the same
  instant but different time zones are not equal.

declarations.comments:
Options controlling how ambiguous inputs are resolved during construction.

- `disambiguation`: how to handle wall-clock times that don't exist
  (spring forward) or are ambiguous (fall back)
- `offset`: how to handle conflicts between an explicit UTC offset and
  the IANA timezone rules
- `overflow`: how to handle out-of-range field values
declarations.title: ZonedDateTimeFromOptions

declarations.comments:
Construct from nanoseconds since the Unix epoch and an IANA time zone name.
Returns `Nothing` for invalid time zones or out-of-range epoch values.
declarations.title: fromEpochNanoseconds

declarations.comments:
Parse a string with timezone annotation
(e.g. `"2024-03-15T10:30:00+00:00[UTC]"`).
Returns `Nothing` for invalid strings.

The UTC offset in the string is validated against the timezone rules.
If the offset is inconsistent with the IANA timezone at that instant
(e.g. due to changed DST rules), parsing fails. Use `fromStringWith` with
`offset: Ignore` to handle strings where timezone rules may have changed.
declarations.title: fromString

declarations.comments: Parse with explicit disambiguation options. See `ZonedDateTimeFromOptions`.
declarations.title: fromStringWith

declarations.comments: Calendar year.
declarations.title: getYear

declarations.comments: Month of the year (1–12).
declarations.title: getMonth

declarations.comments: Day of the month (1–31).
declarations.title: getDay

declarations.comments: Hour of the day (0–23).
declarations.title: getHour

declarations.comments: Minute of the hour (0–59).
declarations.title: getMinute

declarations.comments: Second of the minute (0–59).
declarations.title: getSecond

declarations.comments: Millisecond (0–999).
declarations.title: getMillisecond

declarations.comments: Microsecond (0–999).
declarations.title: getMicrosecond

declarations.comments: Nanosecond (0–999).
declarations.title: getNanosecond

declarations.comments: Day of the week (1 = Monday, 7 = Sunday), per ISO 8601.
declarations.title: getDayOfWeek

declarations.comments: Day of the year (1–366).
declarations.title: getDayOfYear

declarations.comments: ISO 8601 week number (1–53).
declarations.title: getWeekOfYear

declarations.comments: The year that corresponds to `getWeekOfYear`.
declarations.title: getYearOfWeek

declarations.comments: Number of days in this month (28–31).
declarations.title: getDaysInMonth

declarations.comments: Always 7.
declarations.title: getDaysInWeek

declarations.comments: Number of days in this year (365 or 366).
declarations.title: getDaysInYear

declarations.comments: Always 12 for the ISO calendar.
declarations.title: getMonthsInYear

declarations.comments: Whether this year is a leap year.
declarations.title: getInLeapYear

declarations.comments: The IANA time zone identifier (e.g. `"America/New_York"`, `"UTC"`).
declarations.title: getTimeZoneId

declarations.comments: The UTC offset as a string (e.g. `"+00:00"`, `"-05:00"`).
declarations.title: getOffset

declarations.comments: The UTC offset in nanoseconds.
declarations.title: getOffsetNanoseconds

declarations.comments: Milliseconds since the Unix epoch. May lose sub-millisecond precision.
declarations.title: getEpochMilliseconds

declarations.comments: Nanoseconds since the Unix epoch (full precision).
declarations.title: getEpochNanoseconds

declarations.comments:
The number of real-world hours in this calendar day, accounting for DST.
Usually 24, but can be 23 or 25 during DST transitions.
declarations.title: getHoursInDay

Create a modified copy with the given fields and disambiguation options.
Returns `Nothing` if the result would be invalid.

declarations.title: withPlainTime

Change the time zone, preserving the exact instant. The wall-clock time

Add a duration. Date components are added as calendar arithmetic
(wall-clock time preserved), then time components as real elapsed time.
Returns `Nothing` on overflow.

declarations.comments: Subtract a duration. Same semantics as `add`.

Options for `since` and `until`. Default: largest unit is `Years`,

declarations.comments:
`since opts a b` returns the duration from `b` to `a`
(i.e., `a.since(b)` in JS). The result is positive when `a` is after `b`.
declarations.title: since

declarations.comments:
`until opts a b` returns the duration from `a` to `b`

Usually midnight, but may be a later time in zones where DST transitions

modules.name: Temporal.ZonedDateTime

declarations.comments:
A date and time in a specific time zone, with full awareness of UTC offset
and DST transitions (e.g. 2024-03-15T10:30:00-04:00[America/New_York]).

This is the richest Temporal type. Use it for scheduling future events
where wall-clock time should be preserved even if DST rules change.
For past events or timestamps, `Instant` is usually sufficient.
declarations.title: ZonedDateTime

declarations.comments:
A year and month without a day component (e.g. 2024-03).

Useful for representing months in a calendar UI or credit card expiry dates.
declarations.title: PlainYearMonth

declarations.comments:
A wall-clock time without a date or time zone (e.g. 10:30:00).
Range: 00:00:00.000000000 to 23:59:59.999999999.

Arithmetic wraps at midnight with no overflow indication.
declarations.title: PlainTime

declarations.comments:
A month and day without a year component (e.g. 12-25).

Useful for recurring annual dates like birthdays or holidays.
declarations.title: PlainMonthDay

declarations.comments:
A calendar date and wall-clock time without a time zone
(e.g. 2024-03-15T10:30:00).

Use `PlainDateTime` for events whose time zone is tracked separately,
or for wall-clock displays. For unambiguous moments in time, use `Instant`
or `ZonedDateTime` instead.
declarations.title: PlainDateTime

declarations.comments:
A calendar date without a time component or time zone (e.g. 2024-03-15).

Use `PlainDate` for birthdays, holidays, and other dates where time of day
is irrelevant. Do not use for scheduling events — use `ZonedDateTime` instead.
declarations.title: PlainDate

declarations.comments:
An exact moment on the UTC timeline with nanosecond precision,
independent of any time zone or calendar.

Use `Instant` for timestamps, event logs, and any case where you need
an unambiguous point in time. Convert to `ZonedDateTime` when you need
wall-clock representation in a specific time zone.
declarations.title: Instant

declarations.comments:
A length of time expressed as a combination of date and time components
(years, months, weeks, days, hours, minutes, seconds, milliseconds,
microseconds, nanoseconds). All components must share the same sign.

Durations are **unbalanced** — `{ hours: 36 }` is not automatically
converted to `{ days: 1, hours: 12 }`. Use `round` to balance.

`Eq` compares component-by-component (not total elapsed time), so
`{ hours: 1 }` is not equal to `{ minutes: 60 }`.
declarations.title: Duration
